jokes.text: giving everyone 20 minutes to shower, plus 50 for Jim to style his hair, 20 for breakfast, 40 for Erin to get post between her room and the lobby, 90 for Ryan to do his morning ecstasy
jokes.setup: Nellie calculating travel logistics and time allocation for basic morning tasks
jokes.punchline: The escalation from mundane activities to Ryan doing ecstasy as a morning routine
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from mundane to absurd, perfectly character-specific for Ryan's pretentious drug use

jokes.text: I was reading the mattress tag and I fell asleep
jokes.setup: Character asked why they're sleeping in an awkward position
jokes.punchline: The mundane explanation of falling asleep while reading a mattress tag
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist humor with good economy, but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Stanley! Wake up You've got to wake up The hotel's on fire! Stanley, wake up It's pretzel day
jokes.setup: Someone trying to wake Stanley with an emergency scenario
jokes.punchline: Following a fire alarm with 'pretzel day' as equally urgent for Stanley
jokes.explanation: Great callback that shows understanding of Stanley's character priorities, funny juxtaposition

jokes.text: But no kids, so I honestly didn't know what to do with myself. And then I thought of something. Uh Here is Dwight What the
jokes.setup: Jim explaining his early morning boredom and deciding to do something
jokes.punchline: Dwight's shocked reaction to whatever Jim has done to him visually
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim prank setup with good character integration, though we can only infer the visual payoff

jokes.text: at the Alcohol Club
jokes.setup: Context suggests someone explaining Dwight's appearance
jokes.punchline: Calling a bar/drinking establishment 'the Alcohol Club' with childish directness
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor through childish naming convention, but not particularly strong

jokes.text: When I first met Pam she said something that slightly rubbed me the wrong way Since then I've loved working with Pam and she's frankly wonderful but I hate her
jokes.setup: Andy explaining his relationship dynamics and first impressions
jokes.punchline: The contradictory conclusion that he loves working with wonderful Pam but hates her
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific irony showing Andy's petty nature and inability to let things go

jokes.text: I'll have a bottle of the antacid Also I want to get a souvenir for my son but his mother doesn't acknowledge my paternity. Do you have anything for that? Also I want it to have a Florida feel What does he like? Power
jokes.setup: Packer shopping for souvenirs in what seems like a normal interaction
jokes.punchline: The escalation through oversharing about paternity issues to the child liking 'Power'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Packer character moment with uncomfortable oversharing and the absurd 'Power' answer

jokes.text: Okay, Twiggy, that's enough Get in the car
jokes.setup: Someone addressing another character with a nickname
jokes.punchline: Calling someone 'Twiggy' implying they're very thin/frail
jokes.explanation: Basic nickname insult humor, functional but not particularly clever or memorable

jokes.text: Who says none of us are diarrheal?
jokes.setup: Andy complaining that nobody is showing physical stress symptoms
jokes.punchline: Stanley's matter-of-fact response that some of them might actually have diarrhea
jokes.explanation: Good Stanley deadpan delivery with uncomfortable bodily function humor, very efficient

jokes.text: And then I thought of it I'll poison you What are you going to do? Are you going to steal my newspaper or put a cricket in my cereal or something? I'm gonna set your face on fire. That's a good one
jokes.setup: Jim revealing his prank plan and Dwight trying to guess Jim's methods
jokes.punchline: Dwight's escalation from mild pranks to 'set your face on fire' and thinking it's equivalent
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim-Dwight dynamic with Dwight's disproportionate response showing his intensity

jokes.text: Whoa. Stanley, did you just come back from burning down a rival night club?
jokes.setup: Someone commenting on Stanley's appearance
jokes.punchline: The specific and absurd assumption about nightclub arson
jokes.explanation: Decent absurdist visual observation with good specificity in the nightclub arson detail

jokes.text: You're a nice guy, Jim but you have no idea how to vacation
jokes.setup: Stanley and Jim interacting about vacation approach
jokes.punchline: Stanley's blunt assessment of Jim's vacation skills
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Stanley's vacation philosophy vs Jim's work-focused nature

jokes.text: It's so peaceful, I've already written 12 plays today
jokes.setup: Ryan commenting on the quiet work environment
jokes.punchline: The absurd claim of writing 12 plays in one day due to the peaceful atmosphere
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan pretentiousness - claiming impossible creative productivity in typical self-aggrandizing fashion

jokes.text: It's so quiet, one might say you could hear a pin... would be cooler
jokes.setup: Ryan starting the classic 'hear a pin drop' idiom
jokes.punchline: Subverting the expected ending to suggest a pin would be cooler
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection subverting a common idiom, fits Ryan's try-hard hipster persona

jokes.text: Got it Can we see that? Did you really find it? Yes. Right here. Got it. Dink. Ow
jokes.setup: Ryan claiming to have found the dropped pin
jokes.punchline: The 'Dink. Ow' suggesting he hurt himself or faked finding it
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy payoff to the pin setup, but not particularly memorable

jokes.text: Well, you used to be Well, I'm not the receptionist I know but I can't cover reception I have a ton of work to do
jokes.setup: Andy asking Pam to answer phones because she used to be receptionist
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable dynamic of Andy trying to make Pam do reception work
jokes.explanation: Realistic workplace awkwardness showing Andy's poor management and Pam's boundaries

jokes.text: Life is short. Drive fast leave a sexy corpse. That's one of my mottos
jokes.setup: Stanley explaining his fast driving philosophy
jokes.punchline: The morbid but oddly optimistic motto about leaving a sexy corpse
jokes.explanation: Excellent Stanley moment - dark humor with surprising vanity, perfectly delivered and highly quotable

jokes.text: Still queer? You can't put me down Too strong
jokes.setup: Packer's inappropriate greeting and response to pushback
jokes.punchline: His oblivious confidence that he's 'too strong' to be put down
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable Packer moment that's meant to show his awfulness rather than be funny itself

jokes.text: You see, this cat's got nine lives and a nine-inch
jokes.setup: Packer bragging about his resilience using cat metaphor
jokes.punchline: The obvious sexual innuendo cut off mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Typical crude Packer humor, character-appropriate but low-effort comedy

jokes.text: Expected a man, did you? Strong, powerful huge whopping penis? Well, sorry to disappoint But my huge whopping penis is right here and I'm not afraid to use it So stop looking at my breasts and start looking at my penis
jokes.setup: Nellie confronting assumptions about female leadership
jokes.punchline: The extended metaphor of having a metaphorical penis and insisting people look at it
jokes.explanation: Boldly absurd Nellie moment that's both a power play and genuinely funny through its commitment to the metaphor

jokes.text: I went on a shopping spree Very destructive, I bought 1/ pianos
jokes.setup: Nellie explaining her response to not getting the manager job
jokes.punchline: Buying an absurd number of pianos as 'destructive' shopping
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor showing Nellie's eccentric wealth and odd coping mechanisms

jokes.text: What is the antidote? True love's kiss
jokes.setup: Dwight asking for the antidote to Jim's fake poisoning
jokes.punchline: Jim giving a fairy tale answer instead of a medical one
jokes.explanation: Good Jim deadpan delivery with fairy tale reference, efficient and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Right, Dwight. Right, Dwight. Right, Dwight Sorry. Now you'll never be able to get that out of your head
jokes.setup: Jim responding to Dwight's catchphrase about doing something right
jokes.punchline: Jim intentionally creating an earworm with repetition then acknowledging it
jokes.explanation: Clever Jim prank using psychological manipulation, fits the character perfectly

jokes.text: Yeah, it's tender, but it can't be appendicitis. I eat more than enough bacon
jokes.setup: Dwight diagnosing his stomach pain
jokes.punchline: The illogical connection between bacon consumption and appendicitis prevention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - confident but completely wrong medical reasoning based on diet

jokes.text: Send it to the freaking moon, idiot
jokes.setup: Emergency operator asking where to send ambulance
jokes.punchline: Dwight's frustrated and nonsensical response to hang up
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight frustration moment with absurd insult, character-appropriate

jokes.text: Did you say 'masturbator'? I'm a decent baiter. My cousin, Mose, that's a master baiter
jokes.setup: Mishearing leads to discussion of hunting skills
jokes.punchline: Dwight innocently using 'master baiter' without realizing the double entendre
jokes.explanation: Classic juvenile wordplay that works because Dwight is oblivious to the innuendo

jokes.text: It's just rum I'm not bored. I'm a pirate Is that another motto? It's whatever you want
jokes.setup: Stanley drinking and someone commenting on his behavior
jokes.punchline: Stanley's matter-of-fact declaration that he's a pirate and flexible definition of mottos
jokes.explanation: Good Stanley vacation persona - casual about day drinking with pirate identity

jokes.text: I've spent so much of my life telling myself, 'Please, don't end up like Stanley.' And now I'm wondering if I even have what it takes
jokes.setup: Jim reflecting on Stanley's vacation approach
jokes.punchline: The ironic realization that he might not be capable of Stanley's level of not caring
jokes.explanation: Excellent character growth moment with great irony - Jim envying what he's always criticized

jokes.text: Dude, don't you yak on me This shirt is Van Heusen
jokes.setup: Dwight about to vomit during human pyramid
jokes.punchline: Packer's concern about his shirt brand rather than Dwight's health
jokes.explanation: Good Packer priorities - caring more about his shirt than someone's illness

jokes.text: Oh! Who called it? Nothing but net
jokes.setup: Dwight being diagnosed with appendicitis
jokes.punchline: Someone celebrating their correct diagnosis like a basketball shot
jokes.explanation: Good sports metaphor for medical diagnosis, inappropriate but funny timing

jokes.text: Philip, if you're hearing this memo that can only mean one thing, I am dead You are the rightful heir to Schrute Farms. Please, you must do one thing Kill Mose before he kills you
jokes.setup: Dwight recording what he thinks is a death bed message
jokes.punchline: The dramatic instruction to kill Mose in a succession battle
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight drama with perfect family dynamics - treating appendix surgery like a death sentence

jokes.text: Look at these little mini pizzas. Does this make me look huge? Hey, guys look at me, I'm huge
jokes.setup: Kevin holding small food items
jokes.punchline: Using the mini pizzas to create optical illusion of being gigantic
jokes.explanation: Classic Kevin childlike humor - simple visual gag with his characteristic enthusiasm

jokes.text: I'm acting like I like Reception, and I'm a really good actor so people are actually buying that p actually like Reception. But I mean, seriously, Reception sucks
jokes.setup: Andy in talking head explaining his reception work
jokes.punchline: His complete lack of self-awareness about his obvious acting and complaining
jokes.explanation: Good Andy delusion about his acting abilities while being transparently unhappy

jokes.text: Ryan switched his name tag to his pants so now it's like if you wear yours on your shirt you're a total dip But if you switch you're a copycat But I think I figured out a solution
jokes.setup: Erin explaining a social dilemma about name tag placement
jokes.punchline: The absurd complexity she's assigned to where people wear name tags
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin over-thinking simple situations and creating complex social rules

jokes.text: Dwight, will you go back to the hospital? You were there for like three hours. I got the surgery. What else is there to do? Do 100 jumping jacks No. I don't feel like it. You do 100 jumping jacks
jokes.setup: Jim suggesting Dwight needs more recovery time
jokes.punchline: Dwight's dismissive attitude and turning the jumping jack challenge back on Jim
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight stubbornness and deflection, characteristic of his tough-guy persona

jokes.text: You're too slow. You're too small. Seabiscuit is a stupid name You guys sound just like the enemies of Seabiscuit
jokes.setup: People criticizing Dwight's presentation approach
jokes.punchline: Dwight comparing his critics to the enemies of the famous racehorse Seabiscuit
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight analogy - comparing workplace criticism to historic racehorse opposition

jokes.text: The only thing you need to know about retail consumer habits is that consumers are mindless lemmings They just want to be told what to do Fast-forward to today and the retail consumer is independent well-informed, super smart Super smart
jokes.setup: Dwight starting with one view of consumers then immediately contradicting himself
jokes.punchline: Jim's sarcastic 'Super smart' emphasis highlighting the contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good presentation disaster with Jim's perfect sarcastic commentary on the contradiction

jokes.text: The menstrual cycle determines every choice a woman makes. You see, during ovulation a woman's only goal is to get pregnant. During menses she is sad that she has failed. And how does the woman console herself? Shopping. Shopping
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his theory about women's shopping habits
jokes.punchline: The completely inappropriate and wrong biological determinism theory
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Dwight sexism that's meant to be uncomfortable rather than genuinely funny

jokes.text: Mail call His name is Oscar, and he's got some mail And he better open it or go to jail, 'cause it's your taxes His name is Kevin, and no mail for him But he got a coupon for some frozen yogurt
jokes.setup: Andy trying to make mail delivery fun with a song
jokes.punchline: The forced rhymes and awkward enthusiasm for mundane office tasks
jokes.explanation: Characteristic Andy try-hard enthusiasm that creates secondhand embarrassment

jokes.text: What are the Three Pillars of Retail? Convenience Ingredients. Service Burgers. Building loyalty Killing royalty
jokes.setup: Dwight being asked about retail fundamentals
jokes.punchline: His nonsensical answers that don't match the question, especially 'killing royalty'
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight nonsense with escalation to violence, fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: A passionate night with Hugh Grant's brother John Grant He's older than Hugh just a little bit uglier. How did I pull that off? Sheer force of will
jokes.setup: Dwight giving an example of achieving goals through willpower
jokes.punchline: The specific and absurd claim about Hugh Grant's fictional brother and Dwight's confidence
jokes.explanation: Fantastic Dwight delusion with specific absurd details and characteristic overconfidence

jokes.text: You are bleeding through your shirt Oops That's embarrassing Egg on my face
jokes.setup: Jim pointing out Dwight is bleeding through his shirt from surgery
jokes.punchline: Dwight's casual 'oops' and wrong idiom usage for a literal medical issue
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight underreaction to medical crisis with wrong idiom making it funnier

jokes.text: But when the sun came up I knew it wasn't real because she was ugly and I had grown tired of her Reception today but tomorrow you won't want to do her She's a dog
jokes.setup: Kevin telling a story about childhood infatuation to make a point about Andy's job
jokes.punchline: The inappropriate comparison of reception work to a woman, calling both 'ugly' and 'a dog'
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable Kevin moment that's meant to show his inappropriate comparisons rather than be funny

jokes.text: already replaced it with muscle. Can I see the wound? Oh, that's disgusting That's barely healed. You're not paying me to heal You're paying me to kick ass
jokes.setup: Dwight showing off his surgical wound to others
jokes.punchline: His macho attitude about not needing to heal properly to 'kick ass'
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight bravado about ignoring medical advice for work performance

jokes.text: A few hours ago, your body was open like a cabinet
jokes.setup: Packer commenting on Dwight's recent surgery
jokes.punchline: The vivid and somewhat disturbing comparison of surgical incision to furniture
jokes.explanation: Decent visual metaphor with good economy, though not particularly character-specific

jokes.text: You tell her Donna Moraski misses her
jokes.setup: Phone caller asking about Erin
jokes.punchline: The specific name and genuine affection from a random caller
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from the specificity of the name and showing Erin's genuine connections

jokes.text: What's under the cloth? We'll get to that Cookies. I bet you anything it's cookies It's cookies Let me finish my speech first Oh, we get the gist It's just success and effort, isn't it? So just don't be coy. Make with the cookies
jokes.setup: Famous Amos trying to give an inspirational speech before revealing cookies
jokes.punchline: The group correctly guessing and dismissing his speech to get to the cookies
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of celebrity speaker moment with realistic impatience for the obvious payoff

jokes.text: Is it oatmeal with no raisins? I'm sorry to have wasted your time That's okay Thank you very much
jokes.setup: Ryan suggesting a cookie idea to Famous Amos
jokes.punchline: The polite but clear dismissal of Ryan's terrible cookie concept
jokes.explanation: Good Ryan moment showing his poor business instincts with awkward politeness
